# Jeremie Louvaert — Full Context for LLMs

> Open-source developer specializing in ComfyUI custom node development. Founder of AKURATE.STUDIO, a premium brand intelligence platform. Based in Europe, building tools at the intersection of generative AI and production workflows since 2012 (GitHub member).

## About

Jeremie Louvaert builds production-grade open-source tooling for the ComfyUI ecosystem. ComfyUI is the leading node-based interface for running Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux, and other AI image/video generation models. His work focuses on solving practical problems that arise when moving from local GPU compute to cloud API-based workflows.

## Project: ComfyUI-API-Optimizer

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-API-Optimizer
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
When ComfyUI workflows shift from local GPU inference to cloud APIs (Kling 3.0, Magnific, Banana.dev, RunPod), the bottlenecks change from VRAM to API costs, latency, and serialization. ComfyUI's native caching often breaks with external API nodes due to dynamic timestamps and non-deterministic seeds.

### Solution
Three custom node types that integrate natively into ComfyUI:

1. API Cost & Quota Tracker
   - Circuit-breaker pattern for API spending
   - Persistent JSON ledger with per-provider cost tracking
   - Append-only JSONL transaction audit log with timestamps
   - Budget enforcement that halts execution before API charges
   - Uses decimal.Decimal for precise arithmetic (no floating-point drift)
   - Concurrent-safe with file locking
   - Safe budget resets with automatic ledger archival

2. Deterministic Hash Vault (3 nodes)
   - Hash Vault Check: Hashes prompt text, parameters, and full tensor byte content (including dtype/shape metadata)
   - Lazy API Switch: Uses ComfyUI's lazy evaluation to physically prevent upstream API node execution on cache hits
   - Hash Vault Save: Writes API outputs to disk cache with optional TTL
   - Features: recursive hashing for nested structures, device-portable caching (CPU-mapped), atomic writes, concurrent-safe file locking

### Architecture
```
Prompt/Image → [Check Cache] → hash_key + is_cached
                    ↓
              [Lazy API Switch] → routes to cached_data or api_data
                    ↑
              [API Node] → [Save Result]
```

Output files stored under ComfyUI output directory:
- output/api_metrics/api_costs.json — current cost ledger
- output/api_metrics/api_transactions.jsonl — audit log
- output/hash_vault/*.pt — cached API outputs (PyTorch format)

## Project: comfyui-prompt-assembler

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/comfyui-prompt-assembler
Language: Python
License: MIT

### Purpose
Assemble prompts from multiple string inputs with per-slot ON/OFF toggles. Solves the problem of managing complex multi-part prompts in ComfyUI without manual string concatenation.

### Node Variants
1. Prompt Assembler (8 slots) — full-featured with debug output, configurable separators (comma, newline, space, period, custom)
2. Prompt Assembler Compact (4 slots) — lightweight, single string output
3. Prompt Assembler Weighted (6 slots) — weight slider (0.0-2.0) per slot, auto-wraps in (prompt:weight) attention syntax

### Usage Pattern
Connect any STRING output to prompt slots. Unconnected slots are ignored. Toggle slots ON/OFF to include/exclude fragments. Weighted version outputs both weighted prompt (with attention syntax) and raw prompt.

## Project: comfyui-wireless-link-simple

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/comfyui-wireless-link-simple
Language: Python
License: Free to use and modify

### Purpose
Minimal wireless data link implementation for ComfyUI, inspired by Blackmagic Fusion's wireless links. Removes visual wire clutter from complex workflows.

### Architecture
- Two nodes only: Wireless Send and Wireless Get
- Global Python dictionary (WIRELESS_STORAGE) for in-memory data transport
- Universal type support via ("*",) wildcard — works with IMAGE, MODEL, LATENT, CLIP, VAE, and all ComfyUI data types
- Named channels (case-sensitive) for clear data routing
- Pass-through output on Send node ensures ComfyUI execution order
- Negligible performance overhead (dictionary lookup)
